guide.title: Automation catalog
guide.summary: Use the automation catalog to register pack metadata, control install and enable state, track operational health posture, and map outputs into evidence and workflow actions.
body: An automation pack is a governed deployment unit for connectors, evidence collection jobs, or operational compliance tasks.
The catalog top section lists installed packs only, so operators can manage active automations without discovery noise.
Ownership and provenance metadata make each pack traceable to a responsible principal and source.
Output mappings let operators route automation results into evidence refresh records or workflow transitions using explicit targets or scope resolver mode for asset/risk.
Runtime execution supports manual runs from pack detail and scheduled runs via CLI/CRON, with persisted run history per pack.
Automation pack lifecycle, repository, runtime, and output mapping actions are also exposed by REST API under /api/v1/automation-catalog and are published in openapi.json.
Per-target check results are persisted on each runtime execution so outcomes can be audited at object level.
Mappings can optionally propagate runtime status posture into asset/risk targets through a policy gate.
When posture propagation is enabled, asset/risk posture updates keep provenance references to the last automation run and check result.
Each mapping can be gated by execution mode so it runs in runtime, manual apply, or both.
Mappings can also enforce on-fail policy to raise deduplicated findings, with optional remediation action creation, when runtime checks fail.
Evidence policy can be set to always, on-fail, or on-change to control runtime evidence churn.
External package repositories can be registered with trust tier and public key so repository indexes are validated before ingestion.
When no repository is configured, the page opens an onboarding layout with official repository activation and custom repository registration.
Opening one pack enters a focused detail view and hides parent catalog panels until you return to the list.

concepts.label: Automation pack
concepts.summary: A cataloged automation unit identified by pack key, version, source, provider type, and ownership metadata.
concepts.why_it_exists: It establishes a reusable object model so automation features can be managed as first-class product entities instead of implicit scripts.
how_to_use: Register packs with stable keys and meaningful names.
Record source and provenance to keep the supply chain explicit.
Assign ownership so operational accountability is clear.

relations.summary: Each pack has a managed lifecycle state.
Each pack has tracked runtime health posture.

concepts.label: Automation pack lifecycle
concepts.summary: Lifecycle state records whether a pack is discovered, installed, enabled, or disabled.
concepts.why_it_exists: It allows operators to control active automation surface area instead of loading every available pack.
how_to_use: Install packs before enabling them in production workflows.
Enable only packs that are required for the current operating model.
Disable packs that are not currently needed.
Use uninstall to remove a pack record from the local catalog when you no longer want it.
Use the external catalog table to install discovered releases before they appear in the installed-pack catalog.

concepts.label: Automation pack health
concepts.summary: Operational health tracks latest run, latest success/failure, and current health state.
concepts.why_it_exists: It gives operators first-line visibility into whether installed automation is healthy, degraded, or failing.
how_to_use: Track unknown, healthy, degraded, and failing states.
Keep last run timestamps and failure reasons up to date.
Use health posture to prioritize remediation and deactivation decisions.

concepts.label: Automation pack runtime
concepts.summary: Manual and scheduled execution layer that runs active mappings for installed and enabled packs and stores execution history.
concepts.why_it_exists: It moves the catalog from static lifecycle control into actual runtime operations with traceable outcomes.
how_to_use: Run a pack manually from pack detail when you need immediate execution.
Configure per-pack schedule policy (cron + timezone) when runtime should execute on recurring cadence.
Run scheduled batches with the automation CLI command from CRON.
Use --respect_schedule=1 on automation runtime CLI when CRON should execute only packs that are due by their configured schedule.
Review run history to inspect trigger mode, status, duration, and mapping counters.
Runtime evidence mappings now generate payloads via the runtime executor contract and promote them into governed evidence records automatically.
Scope resolver mappings are executed from pack runtime (Run now or scheduled runtime), not from one-off mapping apply.
Use latest check results in pack detail to review target-level status and outcome per execution.
Each check result now keeps direct traceability ids for evidence artifact, evidence record, finding, and remediation action when those objects are produced.
Mappings can enforce runtime guardrails (max targets, payload size) and retry policy (attempts/backoff) for safer execution.

relations.summary: Each execution updates pack health and last-run posture.
Runtime executes active mappings and records success, failure, or skipped status.

concepts.label: Automation output mapping
concepts.summary: A pack-bound rule that maps automation output into evidence refresh or workflow transition execution.
concepts.why_it_exists: It turns raw automation output into governed operational effects without requiring ad-hoc manual copy/paste procedures.
how_to_use: Define one mapping per operational intent, such as evidence refresh for one control or transition for one workflow subject.
Use explicit binding mode for one fixed target object id.
Use scope binding mode for asset/risk selectors (scope id plus optional tags).
Enable posture propagation policy only when you want runtime status to update asset/risk automation posture fields.
Use execution mode to restrict a mapping to runtime-only, manual-only, or both.
Use on-fail policy raise-finding when failed checks must open a finding without manual triage.
Use on-fail policy raise-finding-and-action when failed checks must open both a finding and a planned remediation action.
Use latest check results to open generated evidence, findings, and remediation actions directly when runtime policies create them.
Use evidence policy on-change to avoid uploading identical artifacts when payload fingerprint is unchanged.
Use runtime retry policy to retry failed delivery attempts and track retries in check result diagnostics.
Use runtime guardrails to cap resolved targets and payload size before delivery.
Use evidence mappings to upload or reference output artifacts and promote them into governed evidence records.
Use workflow mappings to apply a specific transition against a specific subject id with explicit workflow key and transition key.
Track last delivery status and message so operators can diagnose mapping failures quickly.

relations.summary: Each output mapping belongs to exactly one automation pack.

concepts.label: Automation package repository
concepts.summary: A signed external index source that publishes automation pack releases and metadata.
concepts.why_it_exists: It enables operators to discover packs from external catalogs without loading every pack as a Laravel plugin.
how_to_use: Register repository URL, repository signature URL, trust tier, and public key.
Use the one-click official repository install when you want a preconfigured trusted-first-party source.
Refresh repository metadata so the catalog validates signature and ingests latest releases.
Review discovered releases in the external catalog and install only the packs you want active in the workspace.

relations.summary: Latest discovered releases are mirrored into local automation pack records.
